QF.id: III-C Fraudulent and Unfair Trade Practices_11

question.text: Trading ahead of customer orders by an intermediary ('front-running') is explicitly recognized under which regulation of the PFUTP Regulations?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As discussed in Case 8.2 (Supreme Court order), trading ahead has been explicitly recognized under regulation 4(2)(q) of FUTP 2003. **The Trap:** While front-running can also fall under general fraud provisions of Regulation 3, it is specifically addressed in Regulation 4(2)(q). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Non-intermediary front running (tippee trading) can be brought under Regulations 3 and 4(1) as fraudulent or unfair trade practice.
